zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Le Clere於1558年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯時，她的父親Jean LeClerc31歲，母親Jenna Lippensen27歲。她於1579年10月10日與Jean Le Mercier在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和10個女兒的父母。她去世於1620年1月17日在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Le Clere于1558年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯时，她的父亲Jean LeClerc31岁，母亲Jenna Lippensen27岁。她于1579年10月10日与Jean Le Mercier在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和10个女儿的父母。她去世于1620年1月17日在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Clere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Clear  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: Edict of Nantes
historicEvents.description: Under the rule of Henry IV of France the Edict of Nantes was issued giving the people some religious liberty. The edict brought an end to the religious wars that were going on at the time.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Reigns of Louis XIII and Louis XIV. Royal power at its peak. France dominates Europe, French culture spreads. Start of large-scale sea trade.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Le Clere was born in 1558, in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, her father, Jean LeClerc, was 31 and her mother, Jenna Lippensen, was 27. She married Jean Le Mercier on 10 October 1579, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 10 daughters. She died on 17 January 1620, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Le Clere est née en 1558, à Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Jean LeClerc, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Jenna Lippensen, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Jean Le Mercier le 10 octobre 1579, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 10 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 janvier 1620, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Le Clere 1558 in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jean LeClerc 31 und ihre Mutter Jenna Lippensen 27. Sie hat am 10. Oktober 1579 in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom Jean Le Mercier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. Januar 1620 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Le Clere è nata il nel 1558 nel Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, sua padre, Jean LeClerc, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Jenna Lippensen, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Jean Le Mercier il 10 ottobre 1579, nel Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 10 figlie. Morì il 17 gennaio 1620, nel Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Le Clereが1558年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンスで生まれた時，父Jean LeClercは31歳，母Jenna Lippensenは27歳であった。1579年10月10日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンでJean Le Mercierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼女は1620年1月17日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウサンプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Le Clere(이)가 1558년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 발시네스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jean LeClerc(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jenna Lippensen(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1579년 10월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 Jean Le Mercier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년 1월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우섬프턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Le Clere nasceu em 1558, em Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Jean LeClerc, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Jenna Lippensen, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean Le Mercier em 10 de outubro de 1579, em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 10 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de janeiro de 1620, em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Le Clere родилась в 1558 г. в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, её отцу по имени Jean LeClerc было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jenna Lippensen, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean Le Mercier 10 октября 1579 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 10 дочерей. Она умерла 17 января 1620 г. в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности соутамптон, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Le Clere nació en 1558, en Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Jean LeClerc, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Jenna Lippensen, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Jean Le Mercier el 10 de octubre de 1579, en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 17 de enero de 1620, en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Southampton, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
